mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals strengthening household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns following positive 3-month RSAFS changes are not higher than returns following negative changes in at least 60% of walk-forward test windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises quickly, higher discount rates reduce the present value of future earnings and tighten financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening labor income and recession risk, lowering expected corporate revenues and margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by above-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases across rolling walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals stronger real output and operating leverage for cyclical firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of the 3-month INDPRO change has a negative or zero rank correlation with 20-day S&P 500 forward returns over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weaker construction activity, household formation, and durable goods demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST decline triggers are not followed by below-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if hit-rate for negative returns is below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with rising policy rates signals a restrictive Fed reaction function that compresses equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change is positive and FEDFUNDS rises over the same 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS tightening trigger does not produce lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than either single-series signal alone in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with a falling unemployment rate signals broad labor-market strength that supports household income and consumption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises over 3 months and UNRATE falls over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down state has a 5-day forward S&P 500 mean return below the unconditional mean in more than half of rolling 2-year test windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth with accelerating inflation signals late-cycle overheating and higher expected policy restraint, reducing equity risk appetite and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises over 2 quarters, CPIAUCSL rises over 6 months, and FEDFUNDS rises over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if the overheating trigger is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations or if interaction terms add no out-of-sample predictive power.
